flashcards.back: Electronic waste (e-waste) refers to discarded electrical or electronic devices that have reached the end of their useful life, including phones, computers, TVs, and appliances.

flashcards.front: List 4 major heavy metals found in e-waste
flashcards.back: Lead, Mercury, Cadmium, and Chromium are the four major heavy metals commonly found in e-waste that cause serious health issues.

flashcards.front: What is the annual e-waste growth rate in India?
flashcards.back: India generates e-waste at approximately 30% annually, making it one of the fastest-growing e-waste streams globally.

flashcards.front: Name 3 toxic chemicals released from e-waste
flashcards.back: Dioxins, Furans, and Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons (PAHs) are toxic chemicals released when e-waste is burned or improperly processed.
Informal e-waste recycling involves untrained workers using primitive methods (burning, acid baths) to extract valuable metals, often in unsafe conditions.

flashcards.front: What are the health effects of lead exposure?
flashcards.back: Lead exposure causes neurological damage, cognitive impairment in children, kidney damage, anemia, and reproductive system disorders.
Mercury causes Minamata disease, neurological damage, memory loss, tremors, vision impairment, and birth defects in offspring.
Indian regulation that mandates e-waste collection targets, producer responsibility, and proper disposal through authorized recyclers.

flashcards.front: What is PCB in e-waste context?
flashcards.back: Polychlorinated Biphenyls (PCBs) are toxic chemicals found in old capacitors and transformers, causing cancer and immune system damage.

flashcards.front: What is reverse logistics in e-waste management?
flashcards.back: Reverse logistics is the process of collecting used electronics from consumers and transporting them to recycling facilities for proper processing.

flashcards.front: What is CRT and its hazard?
flashcards.back: Cathode Ray Tubes (CRTs) found in old monitors contain leaded glass and can release toxic lead if not properly recycled.
An international treaty that controls transboundary movements of hazardous wastes and their disposal, preventing dumping of e-waste on developing nations.
Chemicals added to electronics to prevent fires; they persist in environment, accumulate in food chain, and disrupt endocrine system.
Proper disposal involves dropping electronics at authorized collection centers or take-back programs rather than landfills or informal recyclers.

flashcards.front: What is cadmium used in electronics?
flashcards.back: Cadmium is used in rechargeable batteries, switches, and semiconductors; it causes kidney damage and bone fragility (Itai-itai disease).
